commit.message:
Replace custom Vale workflow with vale-cli/vale-action (#2062)

The Vale CI workflow was ~320 lines of hand-rolled shell that manually
installed Vale, deleted old PR comments, ran Vale, parsed JSON output,
and posted inline review comments one-by-one. All of this is handled
natively by `vale-cli/vale-action` (backed by reviewdog).

## What changed

- **Replaced** manual Vale install + comment management + raw GitHub API
comment posting with `vale-cli/vale-action@v2.1.1`
- **Kept** Node.js + pnpm install and `PATH` setup — required for
`mdx2vast`, the MDX parser Vale needs
- **Kept** `git diff`-based changed-file detection with all existing
exclusions (`snippets/`, `errors/`, `.skills/`, `agent/cli-api.mdx`,
`agent/cli.mdx`, `style-guide.mdx`)

## On the old "comment cleanup" logic

The delete-before-post loop existed because the raw comment-posting
approach had no deduplication — identical comments would pile up on each
push. reviewdog natively deduplicates by checking existing review
comments before posting, and GitHub marks stale comments "outdated"
automatically when code changes. No manual cleanup needed.

## Key settings

- `reporter: github-pr-review` — inline PR review comments, same as
before
- `filter_mode: nofilter` — report all issues in changed files, not just
newly added lines (necessary because Vale line numbers can diverge from
diff positions after MDX parsing)
- `fail_on_error: false` — non-blocking

Note: vale-action v2.1.1 internally passes `-level=info` to reviewdog
when `fail_on_error: false`, so both errors and warnings are reported
regardless of the `level` input.

commit.message:
Phase 2 research complete: 12 configs x 5 scenarios = 900 turns

Results summary:
- Best: multi_ctx1500 and weighted_ctx1500 tied at 92.7%
- Update accuracy: 100% across ALL configurations
- Recall: 75-84% depending on context window
- Verify-update: 60-100% (strongly correlated with context window)
- Larger context (1500 tokens) = +6.1% overall vs 400 tokens

Key findings:
- All graph types performed within 3% of each other (85.9-89.0%)
- Multi-graph slightly best for verify-update accuracy (86.7%)
- Hierarchical slightly worst overall (85.9%)
- Context window has largest single impact on verify-update accuracy
- Update accuracy was 100% across all 12 configs — KG mutation works

commit.message:
Add Settings section to README with auto-generated `flyline --help` output (#281)

Adds a `Settings` section to the README explaining how flyline is
configured, with the `flyline --help` output kept automatically in sync
via a new GitHub Action.

### README
- New `# Settings` section (between Installation and Integrations) with
the full `flyline --help` output in a fenced code block
- Notes that flyline auto-registers tab completion for its own flags on
load (via `setup_autocompletion()`)
- Marker comments bracket the help block for automated replacement:
  ```
  <!-- FLYLINE_HELP_START -->
  ```
  Usage: flyline [OPTIONS]
  ...
  ```
  <!-- FLYLINE_HELP_END -->
  ```

### `flyline_help.txt`
Plain-text copy of `flyline --help` output committed to the repo root;
kept in sync by the Action.

### `docker/flyline_help.Dockerfile` + `docker/docker-bake.hcl`
New `extract-help-text` bake target: runs `flyline --help` inside an
interactive bash session (`bash -i -c`) with `NO_COLOR=1`, strips
residual ANSI via `sed`, and exports `flyline_help.txt` via a `scratch`
final stage. Reuses the existing `built-artifact` context pattern.

### `.github/workflows/update-settings-docs.yml`
New workflow (triggers on push to `master`) that:
1. Runs `docker buildx bake extract-help-text` to produce
`flyline_help.txt`
2. Replaces the `FLYLINE_HELP_START/END` block in `README.md` via an
inline Python `re.sub`
3. Creates/updates an `update-settings-docs` PR if anything changed —
same pattern as `generate-gif.yml`

AI mode improvements: timer, error display, Shift+Enter, gutter char, dparser formatting (#279)

Several UX and correctness improvements to AI mode, covering visual
consistency, error surfacing, and input ergonomics.

## Changes

- **Gutter char on both lines**: `▐`/` ` indicator now shown at the
start of both the description and command lines in `AiOutputSelection`,
not just the description.

- **Elapsed timer**: `AiMode` stores a `start_time` and renders
`Running: <cmd> [Ns]` — updates every ~30ms poll cycle.

- **Error surfacing**: Added `ContentMode::AiError { message, raw_output
}`. Both command failures (non-zero exit, spawn error) and JSON parse
failures now display a red `AI failed: <message>` with up to 5 lines of
raw output, instead of silently returning to Normal. Dismissed with
Escape or Enter.

- **Shift+Enter triggers AI**: New key binding mirrors Ctrl+I —
activates when `--ai-command` is configured and no AI command is already
running.

- **dparser syntax highlighting**: Each suggestion's command line is now
rendered through `DParser` + `format_buffer` for token-based styling
(quoted strings, etc.) instead of unstyled raw text.

- **Quote words with spaces in display**: The `Running: ...` line wraps
space-containing args in single quotes, with embedded `'` escaped via
`'\''`.

- **Refactor**: Ctrl+I and Shift+Enter share a new `start_ai_mode()`
helper.

feat: custom prompt animations via `flyline create-anim` (#271)

Adds user-defined prompt animations: named frame sequences that replace
their name in `PS1`/`RPS1`/`PS1_FILL` with the current animation frame
on every render.

## Usage

```bash
flyline create-anim --name COOL_SPINNER --fps 10 '\e[33m|' '/' '-' '\\'
# Then in PS1:
export PS1='[COOL_SPINNER] \$ '
```

Frames support any bash prompt escapes (e.g. `\u`, `\w`) and ANSI colour
sequences (e.g. `\e[33m`). Multiple `create-anim` calls accumulate
animations in settings across the session.

## Changes

- **`settings.rs`** — `PromptAnimation { name, fps, frames }` struct;
`custom_animations: Vec<PromptAnimation>` field on `Settings`

- **`lib.rs`** — `create-anim` clap subcommand; validates `fps > 0`;
stores frames raw (no pre-processing)

- **`prompt_manager.rs`**
- Animation names are left untouched in raw prompt strings (no
placeholder substitution)
- Added `ProcessedAnimation` struct — each frame is processed through
the same `expand_prompt_string` pipeline used for PS1/RPS1/PS1_FILL at
construction time (resolving bash prompt escapes like `\u`/`\w` and ANSI
colour codes into ratatui `Span`s)
- `PromptManager::new` accepts `animations: &[PromptAnimation]` and
builds `processed_animations: Vec<ProcessedAnimation>`
- `format_prompt_line` uses `flat_map` over `expand_span`, which finds
animation names by direct string search and splices in the pre-processed
frame spans
- `get_frame_spans`: selects the frame index from
`now.timestamp_millis()` — inherits the existing `disable_animations`
freeze behaviour since `get_ps1_lines` already passes
`construction_time` when animations are disabled

- **`app/mod.rs`** — passes `&settings.custom_animations` to
`PromptManager::new`

Auto-close: track auto-inserted closing tokens and delete paired closer on backspace (#277)

Auto-close lacked memory of which closing tokens it inserted, so
backspacing an opener left behind an orphaned closer. This adds
end-to-end tracking of auto-inserted closers using the dparser token
cache.

## Changes

### `TokenAnnotation::IsClosing` — new `is_auto_inserted` field
- Changed from tuple variant `IsClosing(usize)` to struct variant
`IsClosing { opening_idx: usize, is_auto_inserted: bool }`
- Dparser always sets `is_auto_inserted: false`; only the editor sets it
`true`
- Added `Copy` to the derive list (all variant fields are `Copy`)

### `DParser` — `into_tokens()`
- New method to take ownership of the token `Vec` without cloning

### `App` — dparser token cache
- Added `dparser_tokens_cache: Vec<AnnotatedToken>` field alongside the
existing `formatted_buffer_cache`
- `on_possible_buffer_change` now: builds a `HashSet` of auto-inserted
byte positions from the old cache, transfers flags to newly-parsed
tokens by byte-start position (best-effort), then stores the new tokens

### Auto-insert marking
`insert_closing_char` returns `Option<usize>` (byte position of the
inserted closer). After `on_possible_buffer_change` rebuilds the cache,
`mark_auto_inserted_closing(pos)` stamps `is_auto_inserted = true` on
that token.

### Paired-delete on backspace
New `delete_auto_inserted_closing_if_present` checks whether the token
immediately to the right of the cursor is the `IsClosing {
is_auto_inserted: true }` counterpart of the token about to be deleted.
If so, `delete_forwards()` is called first. This is gated on
`!disable_auto_closing_char` and only fires on plain `Backspace`.

```
echo [|]   →  Backspace  →  echo     ✓  (both [ and ] removed)
echo [a|]  →  Backspace  →  echo [|] ✓  (only 'a' removed; closer not auto-inserted after content exists)
```

Flag transfer is best-effort: inserting characters inside a pair shifts
the closer's byte position and the flag is not carried forward — this is
acceptable per the spec.

Phase 2: Fix extraction speed, semantic tags, and validation accuracy

- Add think:false at request body level for sub-second extraction (was timing out at 30s)
- Add hard deadline timeout to OllamaClient (replaces unreliable socket idle timeout)
- Disable thinking for main responses (keeps turns at ~0.5-2s vs 120s)
- Add semantic alias tags to FactExtractor (location→city/live/where, job→work/role, etc.)
- Word-split value tags so 'Austin, Texas' becomes ['austin','texas'] for search
- Only extract facts on tell/update turns (saves ~1s per recall/distractor turn)
- Reduce research matrix: 4 graph types × 3 context limits = 12 configs
- Fix forbidden keyword word-boundary matching in runner
- Fix scenario 3 forbidden keywords (june→june 15 to allow historic context)
- Fix scenario 4 forbidden keywords (remove pixel & ink to allow historic mention)
- Update ALL scenarios with noThink and smaller extraction context

feat: granular safety xfail separating safety from ray/inside failures (#108)

* feat: granular safety xfail separating safety from ray/inside failures

Addresses #104: safety distances are allowed to be conservative in
Geant4 navigation (a larger-than-exact value is valid), so mismatches
between native and OCCT-imported DistanceToOut(p)/DistanceToIn(p) do
not necessarily indicate a correctness bug.  Previously xfail was
all-or-nothing per fixture, meaning ray and inside failures were hidden
alongside safety ones.

Changes:
- FixtureExpectedFailure gains safety_enabled/safety_reason fields
  alongside the existing enabled/reason pair.  Only the two scalar
  safety codes (fixture.safety_in_distance_mismatch and
  fixture.safety_out_distance_mismatch) are demoted when safety_enabled
  is true; ray, inside, and volume errors remain hard failures.
- ReclassifyExpectedFailures now accepts FixtureExpectedFailure instead
  of a bare reason string and applies each policy independently.  All
  call sites in test_geometry_validation.cc and bench_navigator.cc are
  updated; the if-guard is absorbed into the function (no-op when both
  flags are false).
- G4UnionSolid and G4SubtractionSolid are marked safety_enabled only:
  Geant4's G4UnionSolid::DistanceToOut(p) returns the distance to a
  component solid boundary (which may be an interior face), a
  conservative but permitted value per Geant4 navigation semantics.
  OCCT computes the geometrically exact distance to the actual boolean
  boundary.  Ray and inside failures for these classes will still cause
  test failures, ensuring STEP geometry translation errors are caught.

commit.message:
fix: use copytranslate in boolean-compound DRAWEXE scripts to center STEP geometry (#106)

* fix: use copytranslate in boolean-compound DRAWEXE scripts to center STEP geometry

DRAWEXE 'box N N N' creates a box from (0,0,0) to (N,N,N), but Geant4
G4Box(hx,hy,hz) is centered at the origin. Boolean operations
(bfuse/bcut/bcommon) on these uncentered boxes produce STEP solids
offset from their Geant4 counterparts.

Apply copytranslate to shift the boolean result so its geometry is
centered at the native Geant4 solid's bounding box center:
  - G4UnionSolid box-overlap-x10-v1:     -10 -10 -10
  - G4SubtractionSolid box-core-cut-v1:  -15 -15 -15
  - G4IntersectionSolid box-overlap-x10-v1: -15 -10 -10

Also xfail G4UnionSolid and G4SubtractionSolid DistanceToOut(p) scalar
comparisons: Geant4's boolean solid implementations return the DTO of
one component solid rather than the actual union/subtraction boundary,
which OCCT correctly computes.

After this fix all three fixtures show 0 ray and 0 inside mismatches.

commit.message:
fix: use copytranslate in DRAWEXE scripts to bake z-offset into STEP geometry (#105)

* fix: use copytranslate in DRAWEXE scripts to bake z-offset into STEP geometry

DRAWEXE's translate command sets a TopLoc_Location on the shape object
in memory but stepwrite does not encode that location into the STEP
geometry coordinates.  As a result, three fixtures produced STEP files
where the solid was at z=0..N instead of the centred position required
to match the corresponding native Geant4 solid:

  G4EllipticalCone: STEP z=0..30  vs native z=-15..+15  (offset +15)
  G4EllipticalTube: STEP z=0..50  vs native z=-25..+25  (offset +25)
  G4Polycone:       STEP z=0..25  vs native z=-22.5..+22.5 (wrong geometry)

The polycone case was worse: bfuse operated on the two unlocated
frustums (confirmed by z=100/11≈9.09, the intersection of the raw
cones), producing a waist-shaped union instead of two adjacent frustums.
The previously stored expected volume (10211.56 mm³) was calibrated from
this wrong geometry; the correct analytic and OCCT-imported volume is
15409.51 mm³.

Fix: replace translate with copytranslate in all three generate.tcl
scripts.  copytranslate creates a geometrically-transformed copy of the
shape so the translated coordinates are baked directly into the B-rep
and appear correctly in the written STEP file.  Regenerate all three
shape.step files from the corrected scripts and update provenance and
manifest accordingly.

After the fix:
  G4EllipticalCone: 0/2048 DTI/DTO(p,v) ray mismatches (was 2048)
  G4EllipticalTube: 0/2048 DTI/DTO(p,v) ray mismatches (was 2048)
  G4Polycone:       0/2048 DTI/DTO(p,v) ray mismatches; volume correct

commit.message:
Restrict python snake animation to command-word tokens only (#275)

The python snake animation was triggering on any token whose content
started with `"python"` — including arguments, quoted strings, and other
non-command positions.

## Change

Added an `IsCommandWord` annotation guard so the animation only fires
when `python` is the actual command being invoked:

```rust
// Before
if self.mode.is_running()
    && !self.settings.disable_animations
    && part.normal_span().content.starts_with("python")

// After
if self.mode.is_running()
    && !self.settings.disable_animations
    && part.token.annotation == dparser::TokenAnnotation::IsCommandWord
    && part.normal_span().content.starts_with("python")
```

This means `echo "python rocks"` or `grep python script.py` will no
longer trigger the animation — only `python script.py` or `python3 ...`
will.

commit.message:
Merged Fix object state corruption and DoS via ExternalDataInfo attribute injection (#7751)

This pull request implements a comprehensive, defense-in-depth security
hardening for handling `external_data` attributes in ONNX models,
addressing critical vulnerabilities related to attribute injection
(CWE-915) and resource exhaustion (CWE-400). It introduces a three-layer
validation approach in the Python codebase, updates documentation to
detail the threat model and mitigations, and adds extensive unit tests
to ensure coverage of all attack vectors.

**Security hardening for external_data handling:**

* Introduced a three-layer defense system in `external_data_helper.py`
and `model_container.py`:
- **Layer 1:** Only allows a strict whitelist of keys (`location`,
`offset`, `length`, `checksum`, `basepath`) in `ExternalDataInfo`,
warning and ignoring any unknown or dunder keys to prevent arbitrary
attribute or type confusion attacks.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-50aa61e5e475f9182821c60ac6bb05275b8e9aa02661838438e7b7781d31f4b2R28-R46)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-50aa61e5e475f9182821c60ac6bb05275b8e9aa02661838438e7b7781d31f4b2R57-R86)
- **Layer 2:** Validates that `offset` and `length` are non-negative
integers at parse time, raising `ValueError` for negative or non-numeric
values to prevent invalid file operations and resource exhaustion.
- **Layer 3:** Validates at file read time that `offset` and `length` do
not exceed the actual file size, preventing memory exhaustion even if
the model is crafted via direct protobuf manipulation.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-50aa61e5e475f9182821c60ac6bb05275b8e9aa02661838438e7b7781d31f4b2R156-R176)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-6edb59b4b707d7a3c8cf8c52a6dade2991914991bbee68329a0d90889723da43R307-R328)

**Documentation improvements:**

* Added a new section to `docs/Security.md` explaining the threat model,
defense layers, rationale, protected entry points, and test coverage for
external data attribute validation.

**Test coverage and regression protection:**

* Added a new test suite `TestExternalDataInfoSecurity` for attribute
injection, dunder key injection, negative and non-numeric bounds, and
whitelist immutability.
* Added `TestLoadExternalDataFileSizeValidation` to ensure file-size
checks prevent resource exhaustion, with tests for offset/length
overflow and valid loading.
* Updated existing tests to accept both `ValidationError` and
`ValueError` as valid rejections for symlink attacks, reflecting the new
layered checks.

**Internal API changes:**

* Exported `_ALLOWED_EXTERNAL_DATA_KEYS` and `ExternalDataInfo` for use
in tests and for clarity.

**Other codebase maintenance:**

* Added `warnings` import where needed for warning emission in security
checks.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-50aa61e5e475f9182821c60ac6bb05275b8e9aa02661838438e7b7781d31f4b2R11)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-ae6a20a9bd1464d5c21657806635c9d2a15e9c41eb377847b2c729916ac5ddd5R13)

These changes collectively close the attack surface for crafted
`external_data` entries, ensure robust input validation, and document
both the mitigations and their coverage for future maintainers.

---------

commit.message:
Investigate G4CutTubs STEP fixture geometry mismatch (DTI reduction) (#102)

* Initial plan

* fix(fixtures): correct G4CutTubs STEP shape coordinate frame

The previous shape.step had a flat bottom at z=0 instead of the correct
tilted elliptic cut at z≈-22.4..-17.6.  The root cause is that DRAWEXE's
`translate` command stores a TopLoc_Location that is not applied by
`bcommon` or `stepwrite` in batch mode.

Workaround: shift all slab polyline z-coordinates up by +25 mm so the
boolean `bcommon` operation runs with the cylinder entirely below the
slab planes (cylinder spans z=0..60, slab_bottom spans z=-5..15,
slab_top spans z=40..50).  After writing the STEP file, translate_step.py
shifts all 3D CARTESIAN_POINTs down by 25 mm, restoring the correct
Geant4 reference frame where the native G4CutTubs is centred at z≈-0.6.

Verified vertex seam z values:
- bottom seam (x=+12): z=-17.6 mm (expected -17.6)  ✓
- top seam (x=+12): z=+21.2 mm (expected +21.2)      ✓

Also remove G4CutTubs xfail entry from fixture_validation.cc since the
shape is now correct.

Add config to allow disabling dag versioning (#2470)

Adds a Cosmos setting to disable DAG versioning (dbt project hash in
`doc_md`) to improve DAG parsing performance, especially on Airflow 2.x.

New `airflow.cfg` setting: `enable_dag_versioning` in the `[cosmos]`
section (default: `True`).
When False, Cosmos skips computing the dbt project hash and does not
update dag.doc_md. Parsing is faster; on Airflow 3, DAG versioning will
no longer reflect dbt project changes.

## Changes
- `cosmos/settings.py`: Added `enable_dag_versioning =
conf.getboolean("cosmos", "enable_dag_versioning", fallback=True)`.
- `cosmos/converter.py`: In `_add_dbt_project_hash_to_dag_docs`, return
early when `settings.enable_dag_versioning` is `False`.
- `tests/test_converter.py`: Added
`test_dag_versioning_skipped_when_setting_disabled` to assert the hash
is not computed and `doc_md` is unchanged when the setting is disabled.
- `docs/reference/configs/cosmos-conf.rst`: Documented
`enable_dag_versioning` (default, env var, behaviour when disabled).

## Usage
No DAG code changes. Configure once via `airflow.cfg` or with the
corresponding environment variable:

**airflow.cfg:**
```
[cosmos]
enable_dag_versioning = False
```
or

**Environment variable:**
```
export AIRFLOW__COSMOS__ENABLE_DAG_VERSIONING=false
```

closes: #2386

commit.message:
fix: re-authenticate Codex before every invocation to prevent auth.json corruption

Codex CLI is an agentic tool that can run arbitrary shell commands. When a task
such as /init runs, Codex may invoke 'codex login --with-api-key' internally with
test/wrong credentials, silently overwriting auth.json. Since _login() previously
ran only once at construction, all subsequent send()/stream() calls would fail.

Changes:
- Rename _login() logic to _ensure_auth() with per-call invocation
- _ensure_auth() is called before every _create_subprocess() (covers both send + stream)
- After login, reads back auth.json and warns if stored key does not match expected key
- _login() kept as thin wrapper for backward-compat test assertions
- Test: test_ensure_auth_called_on_send verifies per-call re-auth behavior
- Log level changed from INFO to DEBUG (called per-request, not just at startup)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
